description_long: This is a classic cylindrical fire hydrant, predominantly painted in a deep, weathered red color, with a domed top and a wide, flanged base for stability. The hydrant features two prominent side outlets capped with round metal covers, each secured by a short, sturdy chain to prevent loss or theft. A central front-facing outlet is also present, similarly capped and chained, and the chains are visibly metallic and slightly reflective. The top of the hydrant is fitted with a small, hexagonal operating nut or cap, used for opening the valve to release water. The surface of the hydrant is textured, showing signs of wear and exposure, with subtle embossed details and possible faint markings, though no clear manufacturer or model name is visible. The overall construction appears robust, made primarily of cast iron or steel, designed to withstand urban outdoor conditions and provide emergency water access for firefighting.
description: A robust, weathered red fire hydrant with three capped outlets, metallic chains, and a domed top. The surface is textured and shows signs of outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central outlet with a cap and chain, flanked by two side outlets and the domed top.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting one capped outlet and the attached chain, with the hydrant's cylindrical body and base visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hydrant, with both side outlets and chains visible but no central outlet.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite capped outlet and chain, with the domed top and base in profile.
